title: DivFlow: Diversity Decoding Arena — Metric Taxonomy and Systematic Comparison
consensus_strengths: Metric correlation taxonomy answering which diversity metrics agree is a reference result with lasting citation value.
Bayesian statistical framework (sign test with ROPE, bootstrap CIs, Bradley-Terry ranking) is a significant improvement over p-value-based NLP evaluation.
SVD and QD-BS are creative algorithmic innovations from particle methods and evolutionary computation applied to text generation.
1.4M sequence dataset release is a lasting community contribution enabling future re-analysis without re-running inference.
Comprehensive experimental design (10 algorithms × 4 configs × 6 tasks × 100 prompts × 20 samples × 3 seeds) supports robust statistical analysis.
consensus_weaknesses: GPT-2 124M as sole model is a significant limitation — diversity-quality tradeoffs may differ fundamentally on larger, more capable models.
SVD sacrifices SVGD's convergence guarantees in the discrete-to-continuous lifting via embedding-space kernels — theoretical properties are unclear.
SMT-based exact diversity optimization is mentioned but underspecified: encoding, solver choice, scalability are all unaddressed.
No human evaluation prevents assessment of whether diversity improvements are perceptually meaningful.
Score-function approximation using next-token logits as proxy for ∇_x log p(x) lacks theoretical justification.
actionable_recommendations: Specify the SMT-based optimization component concretely: theory, encoding, solver, and scalability analysis.
Add at least one larger model (e.g., Llama 2 7B via quantized CPU inference) to test whether findings generalize beyond GPT-2.
Provide theoretical analysis of SVD's properties in the discrete embedding-space setting — at minimum, characterize when it reduces to post-hoc reranking.
Report which combinations of metrics provide maximal non-redundant coverage — the practical takeaway from the correlation taxonomy.
